quest_intro.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_intro.text: Three raptors. Moving fast. They circle before they strike — always from the flanks. Get to the rocks before they cut you off.

quest_intro.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_intro.text: They have been watching the camp for two days. The young one — the smallest — it scouts first. Kill the scout and the pack hesitates.

quest_intro.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_intro.text: If you survive this, you will know how they hunt. And you will never be prey again.

objective_progress.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
objective_progress.text: One down. Two still moving. Listen — they call to each other with clicks. When the clicking stops, they are about to charge.

objective_progress.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
objective_progress.text: Two down. The last one will run. Let it. A raptor that runs carries the memory of fear back to its kind.

quest_complete.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_complete.text: You watched how they moved. You used the terrain. That is not luck — that is the beginning of a hunter.

quest_complete.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_complete.text: The pack will not return to this ground for many seasons. You have bought the camp time. Use it.

quest_failed.speaker: TribalElder_Kara
quest_failed.text: You ran. I do not blame you — running is survival. But the pack knows this ground now. We must move camp before nightfall.

lore_context.raptor_pack_behavior: This pack of three is a juvenile hunting group — not yet integrated into a larger family unit. Juvenile packs are more aggressive and less predictable than adult groups. They test territory boundaries constantly.
lore_context.why_this_matters: Understanding raptor flanking patterns is a survival skill. Players who complete this quest unlock the 'Predator Awareness' knowledge — raptors are highlighted on the edge of screen when within 40m.
lore_context.ecological_note: Raptors in this region hunt at dawn and dusk. Midday heat forces them into shade. This is the player's safest window for travel through raptor territory.
